agent_output:
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "A logistics platform managing the lifecycle of parcel shipments from creation through transit to delivery, with weight-based pricing tiers.",
    "bounded_context": "Shipment Management — covers shipment creation, state transitions, weight validation, and pricing classification.",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Shipment", "definition": "A parcel delivery unit moving from a pickup address to a delivery address, progressing through defined lifecycle states." },
      { "term": "Pickup Address", "definition": "The origin location where the parcel is collected from the sender." },
      { "term": "Delivery Address", "definition": "The destination location where the parcel is delivered to the recipient." },
      { "term": "Shipment State", "definition": "The current lifecycle phase of a shipment: Pending, In Transit, or Delivered." },
      { "term": "Pending", "definition": "Initial state after shipment creation; the parcel has not yet been picked up for transit." },
      { "term": "In Transit", "definition": "The parcel has been picked up and is actively being transported to the delivery address." },
      { "term": "Delivered", "definition": "Terminal state indicating the parcel has reached its delivery address. Irreversible." },
      { "term": "Weight", "definition": "The physical mass of the parcel in kilograms, must be positive and at most 1000 kg." },
      { "term": "Weight Bracket", "definition": "A pricing classification tier determined by shipment weight: Standard (0–10 kg), Heavy (10–50 kg), or Freight (>50 kg)." },
      { "term": "Standard", "definition": "Weight bracket for parcels weighing more than 0 kg up to and including 10 kg." },
      { "term": "Heavy", "definition": "Weight bracket for parcels weighing more than 10 kg up to and including 50 kg." },
      { "term": "Freight", "definition": "Weight bracket for parcels weighing more than 50 kg up to and including 1000 kg." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "A shipment requires both a pickup address and a delivery address at creation time.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "A shipment without both endpoints is logistically meaningless — it represents corrupt state. There is no business scenario where a shipment exists without knowing where it comes from and where it goes.",
      "testable_assertion": "Any attempt to create a shipment with a null or empty pickup address or delivery address must be rejected."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "A newly created shipment starts in the Pending state.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "State integrity requires a defined initial state. A shipment that exists without a state is corrupt data.",
      "testable_assertion": "After successful creation, shipment.state must equal Pending."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "A shipment in the In Transit state cannot transition back to Pending.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "This is a physical constraint — a parcel that has been picked up and is moving cannot be 'un-picked-up.' Allowing backward transitions would corrupt tracking history and violate real-world logistics physics.",
      "testable_assertion": "Calling any state-change operation that would move an In Transit shipment to Pending must be rejected with an error."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "A Delivered shipment is in a terminal state — no further state transitions are permitted.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Delivery is a physical finality. Once a parcel has been handed to the recipient, the delivery event cannot be undone. Allowing state changes after delivery would corrupt audit trails, trigger incorrect billing, and misrepresent the real world.",
      "testable_assertion": "Any attempt to change the state of a Delivered shipment (to Pending, In Transit, or any other state) must be rejected."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "Shipment weight must be strictly positive (greater than zero).",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "A parcel with zero or negative weight is physically impossible and represents data corruption. Weight is also used for pricing — zero weight would break billing invariants.",
      "testable_assertion": "Creating or updating a shipment with weight <= 0 must be rejected."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "Shipment weight must not exceed 1000 kg.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The 1000 kg cap is a business policy reflecting current fleet/infrastructure capacity. A VP could raise this limit if the company acquires heavier-duty vehicles. It is not a physical or data-integrity constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "Creating or updating a shipment with weight > 1000 kg must be rejected."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "Weight bracket Standard applies to shipments with weight > 0 kg and <= 10 kg.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Bracket boundaries are pricing policy — they can be adjusted by commercial teams without corrupting the system.",
      "testable_assertion": "A shipment with weight 10 kg must be classified as Standard; a shipment with weight 10.01 kg must not."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-8",
      "rule": "Weight bracket Heavy applies to shipments with weight > 10 kg and <= 50 kg.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Pricing policy boundary, changeable by business decision.",
      "testable_assertion": "A shipment with weight 50 kg must be classified as Heavy; a shipment with weight 50.01 kg must not."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-9",
      "rule": "Weight bracket Freight applies to shipments with weight > 50 kg and <= 1000 kg.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Pricing policy boundary, changeable by business decision.",
      "testable_assertion": "A shipment with weight 51 kg must be classified as Freight; a shipment with weight 1000 kg must also be classified as Freight."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-10",
      "rule": "The only valid state transitions are: Pending -> In Transit, and In Transit -> Delivered.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "This encodes the physical lifecycle of a parcel. Skipping states (e.g., Pending -> Delivered) or reversing them would misrepresent the real-world process and corrupt tracking data.",
      "testable_assertion": "A Pending shipment can only transition to In Transit. An In Transit shipment can only transition to Delivered. No other transitions are permitted."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [
    {
      "id": "AC-1",
      "given": "A valid pickup address and delivery address and a positive weight <= 1000 kg",
      "when": "A shipment is created",
      "then": "The shipment is persisted in Pending state with the correct weight bracket assigned"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-2",
      "given": "A missing or empty pickup address or delivery address",
      "when": "A shipment creation is attempted",
      "then": "The creation is rejected with a validation error specifying the missing field"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-3",
      "given": "A weight of 0, a negative weight, or a weight exceeding 1000 kg",
      "when": "A shipment creation is attempted",
      "then": "The creation is rejected with a validation error specifying the weight constraint violated"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-4",
      "given": "A shipment in Pending state",
      "when": "A transition to In Transit is requested",
      "then": "The shipment state changes to In Transit"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-5",
      "given": "A shipment in In Transit state",
      "when": "A transition to Delivered is requested",
      "then": "The shipment state changes to Delivered"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-6",
      "given": "A shipment in In Transit state",
      "when": "A transition back to Pending is requested",
      "then": "The transition is rejected with an error indicating irreversible state progression"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-7",
      "given": "A shipment in Delivered state",
      "when": "Any state transition is requested",
      "then": "The transition is rejected with an error indicating Delivered is a terminal state"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-8",
      "given": "A shipment with weight 7 kg",
      "when": "The weight bracket is determined",
      "then": "The bracket is Standard"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-9",
      "given": "A shipment with weight 30 kg",
      "when": "The weight bracket is determined",
      "then": "The bracket is Heavy"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-10",
      "given": "A shipment with weight 75 kg",
      "when": "The weight bracket is determined",
      "then": "The bracket is Freight"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-11",
      "given": "A shipment in Pending state",
      "when": "A transition directly to Delivered is requested (skipping In Transit)",
      "then": "The transition is rejected — states cannot be skipped"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-12",
      "given": "A shipment with weight exactly 10 kg",
      "when": "The weight bracket is determined",
      "then": "The bracket is Standard (boundary is inclusive at the upper end of Standard)"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-13",
      "given": "A shipment with weight exactly 50 kg",
      "when": "The weight bracket is determined",
      "then": "The bracket is Heavy (boundary is inclusive at the upper end of Heavy)"
    }
  ],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "Weight exactly at bracket boundary (10 kg)", "expected_behavior": "Classified as Standard. The spec says '0-10 kg is standard' which includes 10 kg." },
    { "scenario": "Weight exactly at bracket boundary (50 kg)", "expected_behavior": "Classified as Heavy. The spec says '10-50 kg is heavy' which includes 50 kg." },
    { "scenario": "Weight exactly 1000 kg", "expected_behavior": "Accepted and classified as Freight. 1000 kg is the maximum, not an exclusive upper bound." },
    { "scenario": "Weight of 0.001 kg (near-zero but positive)", "expected_behavior": "Accepted and classified as Standard. Weight is positive, so it passes validation." },
    { "scenario": "Attempting Pending -> Delivered directly", "expected_behavior": "Rejected. The spec implies a sequential lifecycle: a parcel must transit before it can be delivered." },
    { "scenario": "Duplicate state transition (Pending -> Pending)", "expected_behavior": "Rejected or treated as a no-op. The only valid transition from Pending is to In Transit." },
    { "scenario": "Shipment with identical pickup and delivery address", "expected_behavior": "Spec does not prohibit this. Should be flagged as an ambiguity — see escalations." },
    { "scenario": "Weight provided as a non-numeric value or null", "expected_behavior": "Rejected at input validation. Weight must be a positive number." }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Weight brackets directly determine pricing. Incorrect bracket assignment causes revenue leakage (undercharging) or customer disputes (overcharging). Terminal state enforcement prevents duplicate billing for already-delivered shipments.",
    "operational": "State machine enforcement ensures logistics teams can trust shipment status for dispatch planning, vehicle loading, and route optimization. Incorrect state transitions would cascade into misrouted parcels and wasted driver time.",
    "user": "Customers rely on accurate shipment state for tracking visibility. Backward or skipped transitions would destroy trust in the tracking system. Clear weight validation prevents confusion at pickup when a parcel is rejected.",
    "risk": "Primary risks are around bracket boundary precision (off-by-one errors causing systematic mispricing) and state machine bypass (allowing delivered shipments to re-enter the pipeline, causing phantom deliveries and audit failures).",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "Senders (shippers)", "impact": "Must provide valid addresses and weight. Pricing depends on correct bracket classification. Clear error messages needed for rejected shipments." },
      { "group": "Recipients", "impact": "Depend on accurate delivery state for availability planning. Terminal state guarantees they won't see false 'in transit' after receiving their parcel." },
      { "group": "Operations / Dispatch", "impact": "Rely on state transitions to plan pickups and deliveries. Invalid transitions would corrupt dispatch queues." },
      { "group": "Finance / Billing", "impact": "Weight brackets feed directly into invoicing. Bracket boundary errors cause revenue discrepancies at scale." },
      { "group": "Customer Support", "impact": "Clear state machine reduces support tickets about 'stuck' or 'wrong status' shipments." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 72,
    "reasoning": "The spec is concise and mostly unambiguous for the core lifecycle and weight rules. However, several important domain details are not specified: whether Pending->Delivered is allowed (inferred as disallowed but not stated), whether addresses can be identical, whether weight can change after creation, and what the actual pricing amounts are per bracket. The bracket boundaries use informal range notation ('0-10', '10-50') which creates ambiguity at the exact boundaries (is 10 kg Standard or Heavy?).",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "State machine direction: forward-only, Delivered is terminal",
      "Weight must be positive and <= 1000 kg",
      "Three weight brackets exist: Standard, Heavy, Freight",
      "Both addresses required at creation"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Exact bracket boundary inclusivity (is 10 kg Standard or Heavy?)",
      "Whether Pending->Delivered skip is allowed (inferred no, not stated)",
      "Whether weight is mutable after shipment creation",
      "Whether pickup and delivery addresses can be identical",
      "Whether additional states exist (e.g., Cancelled, Failed Delivery, Returned)"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "Bracket boundary '0-10 kg' means >0 and <=10 (upper-inclusive), so 10 kg is Standard",
      "Bracket boundary '10-50 kg' means >10 and <=50, so 50 kg is Heavy",
      "State transitions are strictly sequential: Pending -> In Transit -> Delivered (no skipping)",
      "Weight is set at creation and does not change (spec does not mention weight updates)",
      "There are no additional states beyond Pending, In Transit, and Delivered",
      "The initial state of a new shipment is Pending (not stated explicitly but strongly implied)"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "The spec uses overlapping range notation for bracket boundaries: '0-10 kg is standard, 10-50 kg is heavy.' The value 10 kg falls in both ranges as written. We assumed upper-inclusive (10 kg = Standard), but the PM should confirm whether 10 kg is Standard or Heavy, and whether 50 kg is Heavy or Freight.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Finance / Billing", "Senders (shippers)"],
      "options": ["10 kg is Standard (upper-inclusive boundaries)", "10 kg is Heavy (lower-inclusive boundaries)"],
      "recommendation": "Upper-inclusive is the industry convention (e.g., 'up to 10 kg'). Recommend 10 kg = Standard, 50 kg = Heavy."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "The spec does not state whether Pending can transition directly to Delivered (skipping In Transit). It only says In Transit cannot go back to Pending. We inferred that skipping is disallowed based on the physical logistics process, but this should be confirmed.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Operations / Dispatch", "Recipients"],
      "options": ["Disallow Pending -> Delivered (strict sequential lifecycle)", "Allow Pending -> Delivered (for edge cases like same-location pickup/delivery)"],
      "recommendation": "Disallow. A shipment should always transit before delivery — this maintains audit trail integrity and tracking accuracy."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "The spec does not address whether weight can be modified after shipment creation. In real logistics, parcels are often re-weighed at pickup or at sorting hubs, which may differ from the declared weight. If weight changes, the bracket (and therefore price) may also change — this has billing implications.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Finance / Billing", "Senders (shippers)", "Operations / Dispatch"],
      "options": ["Weight is immutable after creation", "Weight can be updated only while Pending", "Weight can be updated until Delivered"],
      "recommendation": "Allow weight updates only while in Pending or In Transit states, and recalculate the bracket accordingly. Lock weight once Delivered."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "The spec does not mention a Cancelled state. Real-world logistics requires the ability to cancel shipments (e.g., sender changes their mind before pickup). Without a Cancelled state, the only option is to delete the record, which is problematic for audit trails.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Senders (shippers)", "Operations / Dispatch", "Finance / Billing"],
      "options": ["Add a Cancelled state reachable only from Pending", "Allow soft-delete of Pending shipments", "Out of scope for v1"],
      "recommendation": "Add a Cancelled terminal state reachable from Pending. This is standard in logistics platforms and essential for audit compliance."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": []
}
